steps.name: Course Description
steps.description: The high-level pitch for the textbook: who it's for, what they'll learn, prerequisites, and the throughline that ties chapters together. This is what shows up on a syllabus or course catalog page.

steps.name: Learning Graph
steps.description: A directed graph of concepts and their dependencies. Each node is a concept; edges express prerequisite relationships. Drives chapter ordering and the prerequisite hints inside each microsim.

steps.name: Chapter Structure
steps.description: Outline of all chapters with learning objectives, section breakdowns, and approximate length per chapter. The skeleton everything else hangs on.

steps.name: Chapter Content
steps.description: The actual prose, examples, and inline figures for each chapter. This is the bulk of the textbook by token count.

steps.name: Microsims
steps.description: Interactive in-browser simulations students can manipulate to build intuition. Each microsim has a config schema, a worked example, and 2-3 guided questions. Token estimates here cover the explanatory copy, not the simulation code.

steps.name: Stories
steps.description: Narrative case studies - one per chapter - that follow recurring characters using the subject in research, design, and engineering settings. They put concepts in context.

steps.name: Glossary
steps.description: Alphabetized definitions of every term introduced in the book, with cross-references to the chapters and microsims where they're used.

steps.name: FAQ
steps.description: Frequently asked questions surfaced from beta readers and instructor reviews - the recurring confusions and clarifications that didn't fit cleanly into chapter prose.

steps.name: Quiz
steps.description: Auto-graded check-for-understanding questions at the end of every chapter, plus larger unit assessments.

steps.name: Reference
steps.description: Appendix material: math primers, notation reference, an annotated bibliography, and a curated list of papers and codebases for further reading.

steps.name: Teacher's Guide
steps.description: Companion material for instructors: pacing guides, discussion prompts, common misconceptions, suggested in-class activities, and answer keys for the unit assessments.
